mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand that depresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative returns in 55% of co-occurring decline periods or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force rapid portfolio rebalancing and generate negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60% of contraction months or the signal reverses direction during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicate restrictive real policy conditions that compress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.5% monthly while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when inflation rises alongside stable rates, or the interaction effect yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in 50% of months or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% confirms an economic downturn that forces immediate downward revisions to earnings estimates and triggers negative equity market returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in 55% of quarters following confirmed GDP contractions or the signal shows no predictive edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low unemployment indicates capacity constraints that boost near-term corporate profit margins and drive positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in 60% of high-growth low-unemployment periods or the interaction term loses statistical significance.
